perturbation.original_text: This NON- DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (this “ Agreement ”) is made as of this ___ day of ___________, 201 9, by and between Evelozcity with offices at 19951 Mariner Avenue, Torrance, CA 90503 (the “ Disclosing Party ”), and ______________________________ (the “Recipient ”).
perturbation.changed_text: This NON- DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (this “ Agreement ”) is made as of this ___ day of ___________, 201 9, by and between Evelozcity with offices at 19951 Mariner Avenue, Torrance, CA 90503 (the “Information Provider”), and ______________________________ (the “Recipient ”).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Disclosing Party' has been replaced with 'Information Provider'. This introduces a contradiction because the term 'Disclosing Party' is consistently used throughout the agreement. By changing it here, it creates uncertainty if the Information Provider has the same rights and obligations later in the document as the Disclosing Party. The contradiction can be seen in section 2 where it references 'Disclosing Party'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non- Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to videotape, photograph or otherwise record any Confidential Information. The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third parties other than to its affiliates, and its and their respective officers, directors, employees, consultants or professional adviser s (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information for use in evaluating or pursuing a potential business relationship with Disclosing Party or its affiliates (“ Permitted Use. ”) Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (i) hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence, in a manner consistent with the protections it employs to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a reasonable standard of care and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Agreement ; and (ii) use the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for no purpose other than the Permitted Use. Recipient shall remain liable for any fai lure by its Representatives to comply with the terms of this Agreement. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Recipient, either directly or indirectly in writing, orally , visually or by inspection of tangible objects in connection with the Meeting and related discussions or the Permitted Use (including, without limitation, research, product plans, products, services, equipment, customers, markets, software, inventi ons, discoveries, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, hardware, formulations, specifications, product configuration information, product components, marketing and finance documents, prototypes, samples, data sets, and equipment), whether or not designated as “confidential” at the time of disc losure.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Disclosing Party' to 'Information Provider' in the agreement introduction directly contradicts the later use of 'Disclosing Party' in section 2, which defines obligations related to 'Confidential Information' disclosed by the 'Disclosing Party'. The changed text creates uncertainty regarding whether the 'Information Provider' has the same rights and responsibilities as originally intended for the 'Disclosing Party', leading to a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Any non-public information provided by Disclosing Party , including, without limitation, information about the prototype vehicles , component parts the and company itself at the Meeting are deemed Confidential Information (as defined below) for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Any non-public information provided by Evelozcity, including, without limitation, information about the prototype vehicles , component parts the and company itself at the Meeting are deemed Confidential Information (as defined below) for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Disclosing Party' with 'Evelozcity' creates ambiguity. While Evelozcity *is* the Disclosing Party, explicitly naming it here and not elsewhere raises questions about whether only information provided *directly* by Evelozcity is confidential, or if information provided by its representatives also counts. This is contradicted by the definition of Confidential Information in Section 2, which refers to what the Disclosing Party discloses and creates doubt about the scope of confidential data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non- Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to videotape, photograph or otherwise record any Confidential Information. The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third parties other than to its affiliates, and its and their respective officers, directors, employees, consultants or professional adviser s (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information for use in evaluating or pursuing a potential business relationship with Disclosing Party or its affiliates (“ Permitted Use. ”) Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (i) hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence, in a manner consistent with the protections it employs to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a reasonable standard of care and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Agreement ; and (ii) use the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for no purpose other than the Permitted Use. Recipient shall remain liable for any fai lure by its Representatives to comply with the terms of this Agreement. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Recipient, either directly or indirectly in writing, orally , visually or by inspection of tangible objects in connection with the Meeting and related discussions or the Permitted Use (including, without limitation, research, product plans, products, services, equipment, customers, markets, software, inventi ons, discoveries, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, hardware, formulations, specifications, product configuration information, product components, marketing and finance documents, prototypes, samples, data sets, and equipment), whether or not designated as “confidential” at the time of disc losure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Disclosing Party' with 'Evelozcity'. The explanation indicates that while Evelozcity *is* the Disclosing Party, naming it explicitly raises ambiguity about whether only information provided *directly* by Evelozcity is confidential. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' as disclosed by 'Disclosing Party' without specifying Evelozcity. The change creates a contradiction because it introduces uncertainty about the scope of confidential data, specifically whether information disclosed by Evelozcity's representatives is also included, which the original 'Disclosing Party' definition would have covered, thus making the two sections incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third parties other than to its affiliates, and its and their respective officers, directors, employees, consultants or professional adviser s (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information for use in evaluating or pursuing a potential business relationship with Disclosing Party or its affiliates (“ Permitted Use. ”)
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall not disclose any Protected Data to any third parties other than to its affiliates, and its and their respective officers, directors, employees, consultants or professional adviser s (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know the Protected Data for use in evaluating or pursuing a potential business relationship with Disclosing Party or its affiliates (“ Permitted Use. ”)
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' in this section introduces a conflict. The agreement defines 'Confidential Information' extensively, but 'Protected Data' is not defined at all. This creates ambiguity regarding the scope of information the Recipient is prohibited from disclosing. This contradicts the remainder of section 2 where it still references 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (i) hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence, in a manner consistent with the protections it employs to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a reasonable standard of care and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Agreement ; and (ii) use the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for no purpose other than the Permitted Use. Recipient shall remain liable for any fai lure by its Representatives to comply with the terms of this Agreement. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Recipient, either directly or indirectly in writing, orally , visually or by inspection of tangible objects in connection with the Meeting and related discussions or the Permitted Use (including, without limitation, research, product plans, products, services, equipment, customers, markets, software, inventi ons, discoveries, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, hardware, formulations, specifications, product configuration information, product components, marketing and finance documents, prototypes, samples, data sets, and equipment), whether or not designated as “confidential” at the time of disc losure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data'. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' and relies on that definition, while 'Protected Data' is undefined. The changed text makes the contradicted section unclear and unenforceable because it relies on a term that is no longer consistently used or defined within the agreement, hence creating a strong contradiction as it breaks compatibility.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (i) hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence, in a manner consistent with the protections it employs to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a reasonable standard of care and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Agreement ;
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (i) safeguard the Confidential Information, using methods it deems appropriate; however, this safeguard should never exceed a reasonable degree of care to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a reasonable standard of care and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Agreement ;
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence' to 'safeguard the Confidential Information, using methods it deems appropriate' introduces uncertainty. While intending to allow the Recipient flexibility, it also weakens the obligation. The added phrase 'however, this safeguard should never exceed a reasonable degree of care to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature' contradicts the requirement of 'strict accordance with the provisions of this Agreement' later in the same sentence. The Recipient may argue that their chosen methods, even if less strict, are sufficient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (i) safeguard the Confidential Information, using methods it deems appropriate; however, this safeguard should never exceed a reasonable degree of care to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a reasonable standard of care and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Agreement ; and (ii) use the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for no purpose other than the Permitted Use. Recipient shall remain liable for any fai lure by its Representatives to comply with the terms of this Agreement. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Recipient, either directly or indirectly in writing, orally , visually or by inspection of tangible objects in connection with the Meeting and related discussions or the Permitted Use (including, without limitation, research, product plans, products, services, equipment, customers, markets, software, inventi ons, discoveries, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, hardware, formulations, specifications, product configuration information, product components, marketing and finance documents, prototypes, samples, data sets, and equipment), whether or not designated as “confidential” at the time of disc losure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation to 'hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence' by allowing the Recipient to use 'methods it deems appropriate' and stating that the safeguard 'should never exceed a reasonable degree of care'. This contradicts the 'strict accordance with the provisions of this Agreement' requirement in the same sentence. The Contradicted Text is in the same location as the changed text, however, the explanation clearly states that the change introduces contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant the Recipient any license, right, title, or interest in or to the Confidential Inform ation.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary Data shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant the Recipient any license, right, title, or interest in or to the Confidential Inform ation.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' in this context again introduces a contradiction because the agreement only defines Confidential Information. By changing the term here, it's unclear if 'Proprietary Data' is intended to be the same as Confidential Information, or if it refers to something else. This could lead to disputes regarding ownership. This contradicts the rest of the document, as Confidential Information is being reffered to, specifically.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient acquires no intellectual property license or rights under this Agreement except the limited right to review such Confidential Information. Neither this Agreement nor the disclosure of any Confidential Information hereunder shall result in any obligation on the part of either party to enter into any further agreement with the other or to require Disclosing Party to disclose any particular or further Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement creates or shall be deemed to create any employment, joint venture, or agency relationship between the p arties.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information,' which is defined within the agreement. The changed text replaces this with 'Proprietary Data.' The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Confidential Information'. By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data,' the changed text creates ambiguity, as it's unclear if 'Proprietary Data' is the same as 'Confidential Information' and, therefore, is subject to the terms of the contradicted text. This creates a contradiction because the agreement only defines Confidential Information. The change makes the obligations in the contradicted text unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that its breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable damage to Disclosing Party and hereby agrees that, in addition to any other relief available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that its breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable damage to Evelozcity and hereby agrees that, in addition to any other relief available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Disclosing Party' to 'Evelozcity' introduces ambiguity regarding who can claim irreparable damage. The Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief. However, by stating that breach may cause damage to Evelozcity, but not explicitly stating if Disclosing Party is same as Evelozcity, creates confusion. This will create a contradiction, as in the document we establish Disclosing party to be the leading party that can enforce this.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that its breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable damage to Evelozcity and hereby agrees that, in addition to any other relief available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Disclosing Party' with 'Evelozcity' in the first part of the sentence, but not in the second part. The contradicted text has the same issue. This creates a contradiction because it suggests that a breach causing damage to 'Evelozcity' entitles the 'Disclosing Party' to seek injunctive relief, without explicitly stating they are the same. This misalignment of terminology introduces ambiguity about who can claim irreparable damage and enforce the agreement, leading to an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. Since it's unclear if 'Evelozcity' and 'Disclosing Party' are the same, the right to seek injunctive relief is ambiguous.
